Internship

Internship: an Introduction to Business Life & a Development Opportunity

Personally and Professionally

Internships, an essential and complementary part of your education, enable you to experience and develop the knowledge, skills, and competencies you have acquired in the courses.

Here are the things you need to know about this subject:

The student is obliged to read the internship directive and fulfill its requirements.

Click here

for the Internship Directive and Internship Process of the Faculty of Economics, Administrative, and Social Sciences (Currently Internship Directive Article is under review and not available).

Other Documents:

Internship Notebook

:

The student fills in the internship notebook by writing down the work they have done in detail daily. Each working day should be written on a separate page. If necessary, more than one page can be used for a business day, and these pages are dated the same day. (See Internship Directive Article 11).

Internship Evaluation Form

:

When the student starts the internship, they give this form to the institution. When the internship is completed, this document is approved by the workplace internship supervisor and delivered in a sealed envelope, signed and sealed. (See Internship Directive Article 11).

Internship Coordinator:
